data.narrative:
Aalyria, a Series B-stage space and aerospace company founded by Chris Taylor, is valued at $1.3B post-money following $150M+ in total raised capital. The valuation is anchored against 8 private peers in the same sector and stage cohort (Axiom Space, Impulse Space, Varda Space Industries, Skyryse, and Isar Aerospace among them), alongside 5 public space and aerospace comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL).

Two methods triangulate the valuation. The peer-median approach across 8 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private companies yields $1.1B, with a range spanning $274M to $2.5B. The stage-progression method applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B-to-next-stage advancement, implying $2.5B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples is not applicable due to the absence of revenue disclosure common to private companies; the sector median P/S stands at 53.2x across the 5 public comparables.

The two-method triangulation supports a valuation range of $914M (low) to $2.8B (high), with a median implied value of $1.8B. The current $1.3B valuation sits within this range but in the lower quartile, suggesting modest upside to median. Key uncertainties center on revenue trajectory, competitive positioning within the private peer set, and the applicability of public multiples to a capital-intensive, pre-revenue or early-revenue frontier-tech profile.
